data.title: Barrick Gold secures JV approval for Reko Diq project, Fluor as EPCM partner
data.description:
Barrick Gold (NYSE:GOLD) said Tuesday the Reko Diq joint venture shareholders approved the project's updated feasibility study and conditionally approved Phase 1 development capital subject to the closing of up to $3B limited recourse project financing, allowing the project to advance and maintain the target for first production by year-end 2028.

The JV shareholders also selected Fluor (FLR) as the lead engineering, procurement and construction management partner to work alongside Barrick (NYSE:GOLD) in the detailed design and construction of the project.

"The selection of Fluor as our EPCM partner strengthens our ability to execute the Reko Diq project with the technical rigor, operational discipline and socio-environmental responsibility that are hallmarks of both companies," Barrick (NYSE:GOLD) President and CEO Mark Bristow said.

Barrick (GOLD) plans to lock in at least $2B in financing for the project from international lenders, with term sheets signed by early Q3, Reko Diq project director Tim Cribb told Reuters on Tuesday.

Cribb said the mine is in talks with the International Finance Corporation and International Development Association for $650M in financing, the U.S. Export-Import Bank for $500M-$1B, as well as $500M from development finance institutions including the Asian Development Bank, Export Development Canada, and Japan Bank for International Cooperation.

The Reko Diq project, one of the world's largest underdeveloped copper-gold deposits, is located in the province of Balochistan, Pakistan; it is operated and 50%-owned by Barrick (GOLD), 25% by three federal state-owned enterprises in Pakistan and 25% by the Balochistan provincial government.

Barrick (GOLD) hopes Reko Diq will generate $70B in free cash flow and $90B in operating cash flow.

data.title: China's trade war escalation is 'big mistake,' Treasury's Bessent says
data.description:
U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent called China's retaliatory tariffs a "big mistake," contending that the U.S. holds an advantage over China, according to an interview aired on Tuesday.

China relies more on its exports to the U.S. than America relies on exports to China, he reasoned. Earlier on Tuesday, Beijing said it would "fight to the end" in the trade war. On Monday, President Donald Trump said he would impose an additional 50% to existing tariffs on Chinese goods coming into the U.S. if China didn't withdraw the 34% retaliatory tariffs it announced.

"They're playing with a pair of twos," he said in an interview on CNBC. "What do we lose by the Chinese raising tariffs on us? We export one-fifth to them of what they export to us, so that is a losing hand for them."

Bessent said about 70 countries have contacted the White House seeking to start talks after Trump announced a sweeping round of tariffs that imposed at least a 10% tax on most goods coming into the U.S. He also said that countries that did not escalate the trade war will get priority in trade talks.

"If we put up a tariff wall, the ultimate goal would be to bring jobs back to the U.S. But in the meantime, we will be collecting substantial tariffs," Bessent said. He likened the tariffs to a "melting ice cube...because you’re taking in the revenues as the manufacturing facilities are built in the U.S., and there should be some level of symmetry between the taxes we begin taking in with the new industry from the payroll taxes as the tariffs decline."

Related ETF tickers: (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NYSEARCA:FXI),  (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: MercadoLibre plans to boost its investment in Brazil by 48%
data.description:
MercadoLibre (NASDAQ:MELI) announced that it plans to increase investments in Brazil by 48% in local currency this year. The Buenos Aires-based company said the investments will be focused on logistics, technology and marketing. The expansion in the region will also include hiring an additional 14,000 people to bring MercadoLibre's (NASDAQ:MELI) total workforce to over 50,000 by the end of 2025.

The Latin America e-commerce giant plans to spend a total of 34 billion reais ($5.8 billion) on its operations in Brazil this year, which is up from 23 billion reais in 2024. 

MercadoLibre (MELI) highlighted that Brazil represents slightly more than half of its annual revenue, with e-commerce bringing in about 60% of the total and the remainder coming from its financial services unit Mercado Pago.

MercadoLibre (MELI) is Latin America's most valuable company, with a market capitalization of $91 billion and operations in 18 countries across the region. Brazil and Mexico are its most profitable markets.

Shares of MercadoLibre (MELI) pushed 2.7% higher in premarket trading on Tuesday, amid a broad recovery in the e-commerce sector. MELI is up about 6.5% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: ImmunityBio raises $75 million in equity financing
data.description:
• ImmunityBio (NASDAQ:IBRX) on Tuesday announced that it has signed a securities purchase agreement for a registered direct offering with a single institutional investor, providing for the issuance of common stock of ImmunityBio as well as warrants for the purchase of additional shares of common stock of ImmunityBio for gross proceeds of $75 million.
  • If fully exercised, the warrants could result in additional gross proceeds of up to approximately $90 million, said the company.
  • Shares of IBRX were up +1.83% to $2.77 in premarket trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Harley-Davidson says CEO search underway as Jochen Zeitz retires
data.description:
• Harley-Davidson (NYSE:HOG) Tuesday confirmed that it is engaged in an ongoing CEO search process, as part of the Board's succession planning responsibilities.
  • The search process was initiated in Q4 with the retention of an executive search firm after Jochen Zeitz, the current CEO, expressed his interest in retiring from the company in 2025 after five years in the CEO position.
  • Zeitz will remain as CEO until such a successor is identified, the company said.

data.title: Cineplex reports box office revenues of $29.5M for March
data.description:
• Cineplex (TSX:CGX:CA), Canada's leading entertainment and media company, reported box office revenues of $29.5 million for March 2025.
  • Disney's Snow White and Mickey 17 led the box office for the month, the company said.
  • "The tremendous success of A Minecraft Movie kicked off the second quarter with not only the biggest domestic debut of the year but also the best in history for a video game adaptation.  In the first week of April alone, Cineplex has already achieved over half of the box office achieved in the full month of March 2025 and April 2024," it added.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tilray slips after third straight revenue miss in Q3
data.description:
Tilray Brands (NASDAQ:TLRY) traded lower in the premarket on Friday after the Canadian cannabis company, for the third consecutive quarter, fell short of Street forecasts for its Q3 FY25 revenue, despite exceeding expectations with its bottom line.

The Leamington, Ontario-based firm also revised its full-year outlook to indicate $850M-$900M in revenue for fiscal 2025, down from $950M-$1B issued with its prior guidance issued with its Q2 results and in line with $891.9M in the consensus.

As for Q3 financials, TLRY’s quarterly net revenue fell ~1% YoY to $185.8M, driven by its cannabis business, which added $54.3M with a ~14% YoY decline. Meanwhile, its beverage alcohol, and distribution segments outperformed, bringing $55.9M and $61.5M to the topline, respectively, implying ~2% YoY and ~8% growth.

TLRY’s adjusted earnings per share exceeded Street forecasts by $0.03 as its gross margin improved 200 bps to 28% in Q3. However, its net loss ballooned to $793.5M from $105.0M net loss in the prior-year period due to a ~$700M non-cash impairment attributed to factors such as macro headwinds and forex losses.

“In the third quarter, we prioritized sales quality and revenue, protected margins, reduced debt, and improved our capital structure,” CEO Irwin Simon remarked.

Commenting on recently announced U.S. tariffs, Tilray (NASDAQ:TLRY) said its American beverage brands manufactured and distributed for the U.S. market will not face any impact.

The company added that in Europe, its medical cannabis brands and products are manufactured for distribution in Europe and Australia, while in Canada, its cannabis brands cater to domestic consumers.
